title: Synthetic software change review
description: A non-operational example for discussing the schema. It labels a fictional training-demo change and does not authorize deployment or represent a production change policy.

decision.intent: Assign a demonstration label to a fictional software change used in a training environment.
decision.question: Which review label should the fictional training-demo change display?

evidenceRequirements.description: An invented test summary for the fictional change.
An invented rollback note for the fictional change.

sources.title: Fictional training-demo change checklist

citation.excerpt: A demo change with a passed check status, a test summary, and a rollback note may display the ready-for-demo label.

sources.rights: Invented content for specification testing; not an operational policy.

outcomes.label: Ready for demo
outcomes.description: A display label for the fictional scenario, not permission to deploy software.

outcomes.label: Needs revision
outcomes.description: The fictional change is labelled for another editing pass.

outcomes.label: Review the label
outcomes.description: A person should inspect the synthetic inputs before choosing a demonstration label.

rules.description: Label the fictional change as needing revision when its declared check status is failed.
rules.rationale: This exercises an exact string equality without implying production release semantics.

rules.description: Display the ready-for-demo label when the fictional status is passed and both invented notes are present.
rules.rationale: The rule is intentionally narrow and exists only to exercise the draft representation.

escalation.message: Inspect only synthetic training inputs and choose a demonstration label; do not deploy anything.
